message:
fix(ft-nn): MultiLabelMarginLoss composes via pair-wise broadcast [bd-r38w]

ft-nn MultiLabelMarginLoss used to extract input + target values,
compute the pair-wise margin loss in plain f64 with nested loops,
and rebuild a non-grad leaf — silently severing autograd through
the input.

Compose through autograd primitives:
   x_i = unsqueeze(input, 2) expanded to [batch, c, c]
   x_j = unsqueeze(input, 1) expanded to [batch, c, c]
   pair_diff = x_i - x_j
   relu_margin = relu(1 - pair_diff)
   pair_mask = (target_mask outer non_target_mask) — non-grad
              (target_mask = (y > 0.5), non_target_mask = 1 - target_mask)
   masked = relu_margin * pair_mask
   per_sample = sum_dim(sum_dim(masked, 2), 1) / c
   reduce per Reduction enum.

Same per-sample math as before (pair-wise margin between target
and non-target classes, normalized by C), but the autograd tape
is preserved through input. The masks are target-derived non-grad
leaves so the gradient routes only through input as expected.

Closes frankentorch-r38w. ft-nn lib tests: 517 passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
